commands.review_summary:
The PR consolidates the two root Bun Dependabot update entries into one entry and adds a `plugin-inspector` group for `@openclaw/plugin-inspector`.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection and the PR context: current main has two Bun Dependabot entries for `/` with implicit `main`, matching the reported overlapping-directory rejection. I did not rerun GitHub's hosted Dependabot validator.
commands.review_followup: - A maintainer or secops/release owner should decide how to preserve or intentionally drop the plugin-inspector release label while fixing the invalid duplicate Dependabot config.

target.title: Fix Dependabot Bun update config
target.body:
## Summary
- Removes the duplicate Bun root Dependabot update block that GitHub rejects.
- Keeps `@openclaw/plugin-inspector` separate by making it the first Bun dependency group.
- Leaves normal production and development dependency groups in place.

## Why

GitHub rejects two Dependabot entries with the same `package-ecosystem`, `directory`, and implicit `target-branch`:

```text
package-ecosystem: bun
directory: /
target-branch: main
```

That made the `.github/dependabot.yml` check fail with:

```text
Ecosystem 'bun' has overlapping directories.
```

## Validation
- Local YAML parse and duplicate-key validation passed: 2 unique update configs.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `bun run format:check` passed.
target.author: jesse-merhi
